constraints.message: When status is anything other than 'candidate', signatures[] MUST be present with at least one SignatureEnvelope. Pre-admission candidate variants are the only signatures-optional state.
constraints.evaluation_note: Library-evaluated. The TypeBox 8-variant discriminated union already enforces this structurally — the candidate variant has no signatures field while the other 7 variants carry signatures: Type.Array(SignatureEnvelopeSchema, { minItems: 1 }) — so a misshapen payload fails schema validation before this rule runs. The constraint is restated in DSL form for cross-runner parity (Go/Python/Rust runners that may not represent the discriminated union structurally still enforce the signature requirement at the constraint layer). The candidate variant remains shape-only per J3 + ADR-010; the other 7 variants are crypto-bearing and gate via validate() safe-by-default.

constraints.expression: status != 'forgotten' || consumer correlates assertion_id to a ForgetRecord with matching subject_agent_id
constraints.message: When status is 'forgotten', the consumer MUST correlate this assertion to a ForgetRecord (consumer-side state).
constraints.evaluation_note: Consumer-side correlation per ADR-010. The hounfour-typed Assertion envelope does not carry a forget_record_id field — that correlation is maintained in the consumer's assertion-store row alongside the typed envelope. When status is 'forgotten', the consumer MUST: (1) locate the matching ForgetRecord via its own state index; (2) verify ForgetRecord.subject_agent_id corresponds to the assertion's subject; (3) verify the ForgetRecord's authorized_by_signer_id has policy authority for the chosen forget_scope. Library-side enforcement is structurally infeasible from a single Assertion record; the obligation is surfaced via the manifest under reason: 'context_absent' when the consumer does not supply correlation context.

constraints.expression: consumer enforces lifecycle state-transition validity (candidate -> admitted -> {superseded|challenged|revoked|forgotten|escrow|archived}; never {admitted -> candidate}; never {revoked -> admitted})
constraints.message: Lifecycle state-transition validity is consumer-side. The library accepts any valid status literal on a single record; cross-record state-machine enforcement is the consumer's job.
constraints.evaluation_note: Lifecycle state-machine validity is unprovable from a single Assertion record's state — it is a temporal cross-record invariant. The library evaluator SKIPS this rule and surfaces it via the UnverifiedObligationsManifest. Consumers MUST: (1) maintain the assertion-store's prior status for each assertion_id; (2) reject writes that violate the state machine (e.g., 'admitted' -> 'candidate' is structurally unrepresentable in a forward-only store, but 'revoked' -> 'admitted' would also re-open a previously-revoked assertion and is forbidden); (3) ensure forwarder workflows respect the discrete-step transitions. The expression captures the canonical forward path; the full transition table lives in docs/architecture/forget-record-semantics.md (alongside the related ForgetRecord verifiability table).
